mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, which lifts cyclical corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to positively predict 20-day SPX returns (Spearman correlation < 0.03) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above six percent, labor market slack triggers downward revisions to corporate revenue forecasts and compresses consumer spending, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than fifty percent of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and an increase in the unemployment rate indicate a synchronous collapse in consumer demand and labor income, which jointly foreshadow negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS decreases month-over-month and UNRATE increases month-over-month
failure_condition: When RSAFS declines and UNRATE rises concurrently, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative compared to random pairs (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery regimes where unemployment remains elevated above five percent, month-over-month industrial production growth signals operating leverage gains in cyclical sectors and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE above 5%, INDPRO growth fails to positively predict 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts reflects strengthening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts increases are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than fifty-five percent of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below one percent and consumer prices are rising, deeply negative real interest rates reduce the opportunity cost of equity capital and inflate nominal revenues, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1% and CPIAUCSL is rising, the joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns (t-stat < 1.5) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in the federal funds rate exceeding twenty-five basis points signals an active monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and debt service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Following a 25bp+ quarterly increase in FEDFUNDS, SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market where unemployment is below five percent, month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal accelerating wage costs and imminent monetary tightening, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with UNRATE below 5%
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5%, PAYEMS increases fail to predict negative 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC > -0.02) over a 3-year rolling window.
